content: The Fair Housing Act prohibits discrimination in housing based on race, color, national origin, religion, sex, familial status, and disability. Landlords must provide reasonable accommodations for persons with disabilities, including allowing emotional support animals without charging pet fees or deposits.

42 U.S.C. § 3604 makes it unlawful to discriminate in the sale or rental of housing or to otherwise make unavailable or deny a dwelling to any buyer or renter because of a handicap. Reasonable accommodation includes permitting emotional support animals.

Texas Property Code § 92.103: A landlord shall return a security deposit to the tenant on or before the 21st day after the date the tenant surrenders the premises. § 92.109: A landlord who fails to return the security deposit is liable for $100 plus three times the portion wrongfully withheld, plus reasonable attorney's fees.
Texas does not have statewide rent control. Landlords may increase rent at lease renewal with proper notice. Local municipalities may not enact rent control under Texas law.

Under the ADA, service animals are dogs (and in some cases miniature horses) that are individually trained to perform tasks for people with disabilities. Unlike ESA, service animals are permitted in all public accommodations. No fees may be charged for service animals.
